obligation_text: High-risk AI systems shall be tested for the purpose of identifying the most appropriate and targeted risk management measures. Testing shall ensure that high-risk AI systems perform consistently for their intended purpose and that they are in compliance with the requirements set out in this Section.
scoring_text: Skill in designing and executing test protocols for high-risk AI systems, including identification of appropriate risk management measures through testing, consistency verification for intended purpose, and compliance testing against regulatory requirements.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in designing and executing test protocols for high-risk AI systems, including identification of appropriate risk management measures through testing, consistency verification for intended purpose, and compliance testing against regulatory requirements.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in conducting AI conformity assessments under the EU AI Act, including risk classification determination, technical documentation evaluation, and compliance assessment against applicable high-risk AI system requirements.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 9(5) (obligation) equal WIDAI RC domain. RC-S-008 is the strongest match (STS: 0.745, cosine: 0.744). Secondary: RM-S-004 (RM, cosine: 0.730), AG-K-008 (AG, cosine: 0.722).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 7.5/10 (raw 0.745). Shared vocabulary: [against, ai, compliance, high, requirements, risk, skill]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [appropriate, consistency, designing, executing, identification, intended] vs WIDAI terms [act, applicable, assessment, assessments, classification, conducting] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.918 (P=0.911, R=0.924). Balanced P/R — the 31-word competency implication and 27-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.8%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the EU AI Act element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.744 ≈ STS 0.745 (gap 0.001). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [against, ai, compliance, high, requirements] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 23.3%. Shared tokens: [against, ai, compliance, high, requirements, risk, skill]. EU AI Act-only: [appropriate, consistency, designing, executing, identification, intended]. WIDAI-only: [act, applicable, assessment, assessments, classification, conducting].
